questions.q: Pataliputra stood near which modern Indian city?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra was located near present-day Patna in Bihar.

questions.q: Pataliputra served as the capital of which of these empires?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra was the capital of the Mauryan Empire, among others.

questions.q: Which Greek envoy famously described Pataliputra as one of the largest cities of the ancient world?
questions.explanation: The Greek ambassador Megasthenes described the splendour of Pataliputra in his Indica.

questions.q: Pataliputra was situated at the confluence of which type of geographic features?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra lay at the confluence of major rivers, giving it strong defences and trade access.

questions.q: Besides the Mauryas, which later great empire also used Pataliputra as a capital?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra served as a capital for the Magadhan, Mauryan, and later Gupta states.

questions.q: In which modern Indian state is the site of Pataliputra located?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra, near Patna, lies in the modern state of Bihar.

questions.q: Pataliputra is best remembered in history as a great what?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra was one of the ancient world's grand imperial capitals.

questions.q: On the banks of which great river system did Pataliputra grow?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra rose on the Ganges plain near where major rivers met.

questions.q: Which emperor ruled the Mauryan Empire from Pataliputra at its height?
questions.explanation: Ashoka governed the Mauryan Empire from Pataliputra at its zenith.

questions.q: Which kingdom first made Pataliputra a centre of power before the Mauryas?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra was the great capital of the kingdom of Magadha before and during the Mauryan era.

questions.q: Which Magadhan ruler first fortified the village of Pataligrama that grew into Pataliputra?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru fortified Pataligrama in the 5th century BCE, the nucleus of the future Pataliputra.

questions.q: Which two rivers' confluence is most associated with the site of Pataliputra?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra lay near the confluence of the Ganges and the Son, with the Gandak and Punpun also nearby.

questions.q: Megasthenes recorded Pataliputra's Greek name as what?
questions.explanation: Megasthenes called the city Palibothra, the Greek rendering of Pataliputra.

questions.q: According to Megasthenes, the city of Pataliputra was protected by what kind of defences?
options: A wooden palisade wall with many towers and a moat
questions.explanation: Megasthenes described Pataliputra as enclosed by a timber palisade with hundreds of towers and a deep moat.

questions.q: Which Buddhist council was held at Pataliputra during the reign of Ashoka?
questions.explanation: The Third Buddhist Council was convened at Pataliputra under Ashoka's patronage, around 250 BCE.

questions.q: Which Chinese pilgrim visited the ruins of Pataliputra during the Gupta era?
questions.explanation: The pilgrim Faxian described Pataliputra and the remains of Ashoka's palace during the Gupta period.

questions.q: The remains excavated at the Pataliputra site, including an 80-pillared hall, are found in which Patna locality?
questions.explanation: Excavations at Kumrahar in Patna revealed the famous pillared hall of Mauryan Pataliputra.

questions.q: Which Gupta emperor made Pataliputra a principal seat of the revived empire in the 4th century CE?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I of the Guptas raised the family to imperial status with Pataliputra among its key centres.

questions.q: The early Magadhan rulers had moved their capital to Pataliputra from which earlier hill-girt capital?
questions.explanation: Magadha's capital shifted from Rajagriha to Pataliputra as the kingdom expanded.

questions.q: Which Nanda-era ruler is often credited in tradition with shifting the capital firmly to Pataliputra before the Mauryas?
questions.explanation: The shift to Pataliputra is associated with the Shishunaga and Nanda phase of Magadhan rule before the Mauryas.

questions.q: Megasthenes reported the administration of Pataliputra was managed by a city council divided into how many boards?
options: Six boards of five members each
questions.explanation: Megasthenes wrote that Pataliputra was governed by a municipal commission of six boards, each of five members.

questions.q: What does the second element of the name 'Pataliputra' (putra) literally evoke in folk etymology?
questions.explanation: The name is popularly linked to the Patali tree and 'putra' (son), behind folk legends of the city's origin.

questions.q: The Buddhist text the Mahaparinibbana Sutta records a prophecy by the Buddha about which place's future greatness?
questions.explanation: The Buddha is said to have prophesied the future greatness of Pataligrama, which became Pataliputra.

questions.q: Excavated Mauryan wooden palisade remains at Pataliputra confirmed which classical writer's description?
questions.explanation: Discovery of the timber palisade at Bulandi Bagh corroborated Megasthenes' account of the city walls.

questions.q: Which archaeologist conducted important early excavations at Pataliputra in the early twentieth century?
questions.explanation: D. B. Spooner led notable excavations at Kumrahar and Bulandi Bagh, uncovering Mauryan Pataliputra.

questions.q: The pillared hall unearthed at Kumrahar is often compared in style to which foreign tradition?
questions.explanation: The Kumrahar pillared hall is often compared with Achaemenid Persian audience halls like those at Persepolis.

questions.q: By the time of the medieval period, Pataliputra had largely declined, partly due to repeated flooding from which river?
options: The Son and shifting river courses
questions.explanation: Shifting river courses and flooding contributed to Pataliputra's decline in the post-Gupta centuries.

questions.q: The Greek account states Pataliputra's palisade had roughly how many gates?
questions.explanation: Megasthenes reported the wooden wall of Pataliputra was pierced by sixty-four gates and 570 towers.

questions.q: The Patali tree, from which the city's name is traditionally derived, is which kind of plant?
questions.explanation: The Patali (Stereospermum) is a flowering trumpet-flower tree said to lend the city its name.

questions.q: Which later ruler is associated with renewed activity at the Pataliputra region before its eclipse by other centres?
options: The Cholas of the far south
The Pala and Gupta successors of the region
questions.explanation: After the Guptas, regional successors such as the Palas remained connected to the area before Pataliputra's eclipse.
